html:div.html:p: A displayed category \overline {\mathcal {D}} over \mathcal {C} assigns to each object x \in \mathcal {C} a family of objects \overline {\mathcal {D}}_{x} over x, and to each morphism f : x \to y, \overline {x} \in \overline {\mathcal {D}}_{x}, \overline {y} \in \overline {\mathcal {D}}_{y}, a family of morphisms \overline {\mathcal {D}}_{f}{\mathopen {}\left [\overline {x} , \overline {y}\right ]\mathclose {}} over f
It is a category varying over \mathcal {C}

html:div.style: border-left: 4px solid var(--accent); background: var(--bg-subtle, rgba(0,0,0,0.03)); padding: 0.7em 1em; margin: 0.8em 0; border-radius: 2px

html:div.html:div: A category is to a context as a displayed category is to a dependent type

html:div.html:p: A section of \overline {\mathcal {D}} picks, for each x \in \mathcal {C}, an object s(x) over x, and for each f : x \to y a morphism s(f) : s(x) \to s(y) over f --- functorially

html:div.style: border-left: 4px solid var(--accent); background: var(--bg-subtle, rgba(0,0,0,0.03)); padding: 0.7em 1em; margin: 0.8em 0; border-radius: 2px
color: var(--accent); font-variant: small-caps; letter-spacing: 0.12em; font-size: 0.85em; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 0.3em

html:div.html:div: A proof of canonicity = a section of the displayed category of canonical forms

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The total category \int \overline {\mathcal {D}} of a displayed category \overline {\mathcal {D}} over \mathcal {C} collects the displayed data into a single category. Its objects are pairs of an object x of \mathcal {C} with an object \overline {x} over it, and its morphisms {\mathopen {}\left (x, \overline {x}\right )\mathclose {}} \to {\mathopen {}\left (y, \overline {y}\right )\mathclose {}} are pairs of a morphism f : x \to y with a displayed morphism \overline {f} over f.

Projecting out the first components is a functor \int \overline {\mathcal {D}} \to \mathcal {C}. Constructions presented displayed — algebras, Eilenberg–Moore categories — get their forgetful functor for free as this projection.
Fix an endofunctor F : \mathcal {C} \to \mathcal {C}. The F-algebras form a displayed category \mathrm {AlgStr}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} over \mathcal {C}.
The total category \mathrm {Alg}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} = \int \mathrm {AlgStr}{\mathopen {}\left (F\right )\mathclose {}} is the category of F-algebras.
